example.vi: Ơ, bạn có thấy cái này không?

definitions.es: Una interjección utilizada para expresar sorpresa o para llamar la atención de alguien.
definitions.en: An interjection used to express surprise or to get someone's attention.
definitions.vi: Một từ cảm thán dùng để diễn tả sự ngạc nhiên hoặc để thu hút sự chú ý của ai đó.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Uh, I forgot to reply to your message.
Uh, isn't this the shirt you picked?
Uh, why has everyone already left?
notes_md:
**ơ** = “uh?”, “oh?”, “huh?” — a quick sound you make when you’re surprised, confused, or just noticed something.

It’s an interjection, usually at the beginning of a sentence, followed by a comma. It shows sudden realization, mild confusion, or “wait, what?” energy. Tone matters: short and sharp = surprise; longer = confusion.

In the South, people often say **“ủa”** instead — same feeling, different sound.

Think of it as the Vietnamese version of “Oh?” or “Huh?” when something doesn’t match your expectation.

It’s an interjection, usually at the beginning of a sentence, followed by a comma. It shows sudden realization, mild confusion, or “wait, what?” energy. Tone matters: short and sharp = surprise; longer = confusion.

In the South, people often say “ủa” instead — same feeling, different sound.

Think of it as the Vietnamese version of “Oh?” or “Huh?” when something doesn’t match your expectation.
exampleSentences: Ơ, anh quên chưa trả lời tin nhắn của em rồi.
Ơ, đây không phải là chiếc áo mà chị đã chọn sao?
Ơ, tại sao mọi người lại đi về hết rồi nhỉ?
